name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
description: Adversaries disable or modify security tools to avoid detection of their activities. In Azure/Entra ID environments, this includes disabling Microsoft Defender for Cloud plans, removing diagnostic settings (log forwarding to Log Analytics), disabling Azure Policy assignments, modifying Conditional Access policies, or removing Microsoft Defender for Endpoint onboarding from Azure VMs. AuditLogs captures Entra ID policy changes while AzureActivity captures Defender for Cloud and diagnostic setting modifications.
iocs: AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.SECURITY/PRICINGS/WRITE' setting pricing tier to 'Free' — disabling Defender for Cloud plans
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.INSIGHTS/DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE' removing diagnostic settings that forward logs to Log Analytics workspace
AuditLogs OperationName 'Update conditional access policy' setting policy state from 'enabled' to 'disabled' or adding broad exclusions
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.AUTHORIZATION/POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE' removing Azure Policy assignments that enforce security controls
AuditLogs OperationName 'Delete conditional access policy' or bulk CA policy modifications in a short window
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.COMPUTE/VIRTUALMACHINES/EXTENSIONS/DELETE' removing the MDE extension from VMs — endpoint protection removal
AzureActivity 'MICROSOFT.SECURITY/AUTOMATIONS/WRITE' modifying or disabling Defender for Cloud workflow automations and alert suppressions
evasion_variants: Modify diagnostic settings to forward logs to an attacker-controlled storage account instead of deleting them — appears as a configuration change rather than a deletion
Add a CA policy exclusion for a specific IP range (attacker-controlled proxy) rather than disabling the policy entirely
Disable Defender for Cloud at the subscription scope level rather than per resource to reduce the number of AzureActivity events generated
Use a PIM-eligible role activated briefly for the defense modification, then deactivate the role to reduce the persistent privilege footprint
Modify alert notification settings to redirect Defender for Cloud alerts to a non-monitored email address rather than deleting alert rules
Reduce diagnostic settings log retention to 1 day rather than deleting them — evidence destruction with lower detection probability
detection_kql_hints: AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue has_any ('DIAGNOSTICSETTINGS/DELETE','PRICINGS/WRITE','POLICYASSIGNMENTS/DELETE') | project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerIpAddress, OperationNameValue, ResourceId

AuditLogs | where OperationName has_any ('conditional access policy','Update policy','Delete policy') | where ResultDescription != 'None' | project TimeGenerated, OperationName, InitiatedBy, TargetResources
AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue == 'MICROSOFT.SECURITY/PRICINGS/WRITE' | where Properties has '"pricingTier":"Free"' | project TimeGenerated, Caller, ResourceId, Properties

AzureActivity | where OperationNameValue has 'EXTENSIONS/DELETE' | where Properties has_any ('MDE','MicrosoftDefender','AzureSecurityLinuxAgent','AzureSecurityWindowsAgent') | project TimeGenerated, Caller, ResourceId

AuditLogs | where OperationName == 'Update conditional access policy' | extend PolicyId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id) | mv-expand ModifiedProperties = TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties | where ModifiedProperties.displayName == 'State' and ModifiedProperties.newValue == '"disabled"'
